title: Huevos Rancheros

overview.description: Lightly fried tortillas topped with ranchero tomato sauce, fried eggs and fresh garnishes.

ingredients: 1 jalapeño or green chilli, finely chopped
method: Heat the olive oil in a saucepan over a medium heat.
Add the garlic, chilli, cumin and oregano and cook for 1 minute.
Simmer for 15 minutes until slightly thickened.
Meanwhile, lightly fry the tortillas for 20–30 seconds per side until warmed and just beginning to crisp.
Fry the eggs until the whites are set and the yolks remain runny.
Place each tortilla onto a plate.
Finish with fresh coriander and crumbled cheese if using.

learning.did-you-know: Huevos Rancheros means 'Rancher's Eggs' and was traditionally eaten as a hearty breakfast by farm workers in rural Mexico.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a simple ranchero sauce and combine crisp tortillas with fried eggs for contrasting textures.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A colourful breakfast that makes excellent use of homemade tortillas and pantry staples while introducing another classic world breakfast.
chapman-tips: Don't over-fry the tortillas—they should still be flexible enough to cut easily.
A runny yolk enriches the tomato sauce beautifully.
Make extra ranchero sauce and freeze it for future breakfasts.
Fresh coriander and a squeeze of lime brighten the whole dish.
Warm the plates while cooking. Huevos Rancheros cools surprisingly quickly, and warm plates keep the eggs and sauce at their best.
notes.batch-cooking: The ranchero sauce freezes well in meal-sized portions.
notes.leftovers: Leftover sauce is excellent with grilled chicken, rice or as a filling for burritos.
